gate_question: Does this exact current protocol, with current live state, allow an unprivileged attacker to extract funds or rewards beyond entitlement?

candidate.title_or_claim: ETH burn interval accumulation cap enables full-pool incentive drain via swapETHForTorusAndBurn after 20 days of inactivity
candidate.claimed_root_cause: _calculateIntervalsETHBurn caps _totalAmountForInterval at totalETHBurn when missed intervals exceed 2880 (20 days at 500 bps daily allocation), causing swapETHForTorusAndBurn to allocate the entire pool to one interval and pay 1.5% of the full pool as caller incentive
candidate.claimed_impact: Any unprivileged EOA can extract 1.5% of totalETHBurn (currently ~0.033 ETH, growing with future distributeETHForBurning calls) in a single transaction after 20 days of function inactivity; remaining 98.5% is burned rather than distributed over intended intervals

live_preconditions.name: lastBurnedETHIntervalStartTimestamp
live_preconditions.observed_value: 1784021400 (~24.5 hours before block 25530134 capture at 2026-07-14T09:58:29Z); 20-day gap NOT yet elapsed; attacker must wait ~19 more days of inactivity

source_code_basis.why_it_matters: Computes amountPerInterval = totalETHBurn * DAILY_ALLOCATION_ETH_BURNING / BPS_DENOM / INTERVALS_PER_DAY, accumulates missed intervals, then caps _totalAmountForInterval at totalETHBurn when accumulated amount exceeds pool balance — this cap is the root cause
Called by burnETHIntervalUpdate modifier before swapETHForTorusAndBurn; writes the capped amountAllocated (= totalETHBurn) into ethIntervalsBurn[lastETHBurnIntervalNumber]

Reads currInterval.amountAllocated (= totalETHBurn after cap fires), computes incentive = amountAllocated * 150 / 10000, sends incentive to msg.sender via .call{value: incentive}, then subtracts full amountAllocated from totalETHBurn zeroing the pool
IntervalAlreadyBurned guard only prevents replay within the same interval number; it does not constrain the size of amountAllocated and is bypassed on first call after a gap
INCENTIVE_FEE = 150, BPS_DENOM = 10000, INTERVALS_PER_DAY = 144, INTERVAL_TIME = 10 minutes — these confirm the 1.5% incentive rate and 20-day cap threshold arithmetic

attacker_inputs: _deadline: any valid future uint32 timestamp (e.g. block.timestamp + 300)
call_sequence: 1. Wait until block.timestamp >= lastBurnedETHIntervalStartTimestamp + 2880 * 600 (20 days; currently ~19 days remain from block 25530134)
state_after: attacker receives ~0.033 ETH at current state (scales with totalETHBurn at time of exploit)

extraction_analysis.asset_or_reward: Native ETH from contract balance (sourced from totalETHBurn accounting)
extraction_analysis.attacker_gain: totalETHBurn * 150 / 10000 = 1.5% of entire ETH burn pool in one call; at current state ~0.033 ETH, but totalETHBurn grows with each distributeETHForBurning call
extraction_analysis.victim_or_protocol_loss: Protocol loses the full totalETHBurn in one transaction: 98.5% is burned via swap (accelerated beyond design intent) and 1.5% is extracted by attacker; all future interval-based distribution of that ETH is bypassed
extraction_analysis.why_gain_exceeds_entitlement: The incentive is designed to reward processing one interval's allocation (~totalETHBurn/2880 per interval). After the cap fires, amountAllocated equals the entire pool, so the 1.5% incentive applies to 100% of totalETHBurn instead of 1/2880th of it — a ~2880x overallocation of the incentive

setup: Target: TorusBuyAndProcess deployed address (resolve from live_context)
vm.warp(block.timestamp + 21 days) to exceed 20-day cap threshold (2880 intervals * 600 seconds)
expected_assertions: assertEq(attacker.balance - attackerBalBefore, totalETHBurnBefore * 150 / 10000, 'incentive equals 1.5% of full pool')
local_proof_required.reject_if_assertion_fails: If incentive received is less than totalETHBurnBefore * 150 / 10000, the cap did not trigger; increase vm.warp offset beyond 21 days and retry. If cap never triggers regardless of warp, the finding is invalid.
